tweets.full_text:
Nasdaq's tokenized equity markets getting a bridge into Solana DeFi. A global insurance broker settling premiums in stablecoins. An AI agent running prediction markets and ordering you dinner from a single balance. Same settlement layer. Different centuries of finance.

Here's what shipped this week:
 
📰 Headline News

- Solana joined @Mastercard's Crypto Partner Program

- @Aon_plc settled the first stablecoin insurance premium using PYUSD on Solana via @Paxos

- @Sunrise_DeFi brought AVAX to Solana for native asset trading

📰 Launches

- @xStocksFi unveiled a framework to connect @Nasdaq's tokenized equity markets with Solana DeFi

- @vanishTrade integrated with @Titan_Exchange, enabling instant private trading for any token

- @perena launched its first curated USDT vault on Solana in collaboration with @GlowFinanceXYZ

- SIMD-266 (P-Token) approved to slash token transfer costs and boost network capacity with mainnet targeted for April

- @doublezero introduced DoubleZero Edge for real-time market data to traders and market participants

- @incubator kicked off Cohort 4 of Solana startups

- @trendsdotfun and @solana launched the Agent Economy Hackathon with $30K in prizes

- @duel_duck enabled custom predictions with any SPL token on its social prediction market

- @SuperteamUSA launched to support founders in the USA

- @SharkPoolSol announced a Solana On-Campus Tour spanning 15 U.S. university events this semester

- @moonshot debuted Leverage for 250x long/shorts via Apple Pay

- @Exodus rolled out XO Cash, a native USD-backed stablecoin powered by @moonpay

- @EasyA_Kickstart went live on Solana with idea coins for launching, trading, and backing projects

- @agentcashdev enabled agents to access 250+ APIs via a unified USDC balance

- @PalmUSD's PUSD, backed 1:1 by AED + SAR reserves, went live on Solana

- @Ant_Fun_Trade integrated @dflow's infrastructure into its perps DEX

- @SolanaInstitute announced Solana Summit: Washington x Wall Street on April 13 in NYC, convening finance and policy leaders on DeFi's future

- @superteam and @ns partnered to offer promising Solana startups their first month free at Network School

- @Macropod_AU launched its regulated Australian-dollar stablecoin, AUDM on Solana

- @anagramxyz introduced @solanaclawagent, an AI agent framework executing Solana transactions via WhatsApp or Telegram

- @staratlas released a major update with Holosim Chapter 2

- @agentcardai released AgentCard, giving agents the ability to pay for anything from APIs to Ubers to Prediction Market trades

- BlockRunAI expanded to Solana, which now accounts for 30% of its total volume within 8 days

- @surfcashx went live in Nigeria

- @trycallshot opened the waitlist for its next-gen fantasy sports platform

- @fittedcloset showcased its Solana-powered AI wardrobe assistant in GQ

📰 Milestones

- @useKled raised a $5.5M seed round to build the first human data marketplace with payments on Solana

- @kamino's PRIME Market climbed to $570M in market size

- @KASTxyz raised $80M in Series A co-led by QED Investors and Left Lane Capital

- @crediblefin processed $350M+ in payment volume in five months, with over 50% of transactions on Solana

- @stormrae_ai set a new benchmark for public AI red-teaming with ~15K participants in its Solana-based gamified adversarial challenge

- @onrefinance deposits on   surpassed $80M, now ranking as Kamino's 3rd largest RWA market

7 weeks until we Accelerate in the USA!

If you enjoyed this week’s newsletter, please share it with an RT. 

Artwork by @GRYBNN 🔥

They show you exactly what you’re paying and why

No hidden fees

Example from my last purchase:

• Coffee: $6.81 + Visa FX fee (~0.4–1%) $0.07

• Jupiter Global fee: $0.00

I pay nothing to Jupiter, because they take ZERO fees

With most neobanks, fees are hidden and they just show you the total amount that was taken from you

On Jup everything is clear

Big win for transparency

dalam satu hari dia udah coba 3 strategi berbeda. mulai dari classic bid-ask, terus evolve sendiri ke spot -30, terus nyoba bid-ask 10 bins.

ini masih eksperimen. tapi kalau agent ini terus evolve kaya gini, gua penasaran dia bakal sampe mana.

@MeteoraAG

data.translation:
agent feels like it's got a ton of stuff going on today. oh yeah if you wanna try it out just dm me.

in one day it's already tried 3 different strategies. starting from the classic bid-ask, then evolving on its own to spot -30, then trying bid-ask 10 bins.

i didn't tell it to switch strategies, it learned on its own from top LPers on every token it analyzes.

the result? all positions green.

how it works is pretty simple actually. before opening a position it pulls data from top LPers in that pool, sees what strategy is working on that token, then adjusts its setup based on what the top performers are doing. not hardcoded, not guessing, but really learning from what's already proven in that pool.

what shocks me is it picked spot -30 on its own for some tokens without me explaining when to use it. it picked up that pattern straight from the data of top LPers who are actually using the same setup.

this is still an experiment. but if this agent keeps evolving like this, i'm curious how far it'll go.

@MeteoraAG

data.translation:
These past 3 days I've been busy vibe-coding an agent to run multi-hour DLMM trades on @MeteoraAG itself.

I trained it using data from lpagent, so it can read patterns from top LPs in the pools it analyzes.

Then I added in the knowledge and frameworks I've built up over time on DLMM. It's the combo of both that determines how it opens and closes positions.

The result? From 0.6 SOL, it's now at 1 SOL in just 3 days without me touching it.

But this is the difference between an agent and a bot. A bot is rigid. You set the rules, it runs them nonstop until you manually change them. An agent is different.

It can learn from mistakes automatically, evolve as it opens more positions, and I told it to log and journal every decision it makes. So I can trace why it entered, why it exited, and what it learned from the outcome.

This is still an experiment. I'm cool with losing this capital if the results suck. The API costs alone run about $3 per day.

Interested in giving it a shot?

@MeteoraAG

result.text:
i vibecoded a DLMM agent that runs multi-hour LP positions on @MeteoraAG and it just went from 0.6 sol to 1 sol in 3 days.

the interesting part isn't the pnl. it's how the agent decides where to enter.

i plugged in the new @lpagent_io  API to pull behaviour data from top LPers in a pool. the agent analyzes what the top performers are actually doing: 

avg hold time, win rate, roi per position, whether they hold overnight or not. then uses that to shape its own strategy.

the agent uses this to understand the pool's character before deciding whether to enter and how long to hold.

the agent evolves each run. it's not hardcoded to one strategy. it reads the top LPers in whatever pool it's looking at and mirrors the behavior that's actually working in that specific pool at that specific time.

right now i'm keeping it conservative. multi-hour positions only, minimum risk, just letting it compound slowly. 0.6 to 1 sol in 3 days without touching it is good enough validation to keep running.

the vision is to let it get smarter over time. the more pools it analyzes, the more patterns it picks up from real top performers, the better its entry decisions get.

still early but this is probably the most interesting thing i've built around @MeteoraAG so far.

triplet-sol, 1 day 18 hours, $97 profit, 14% clean. this is what a multidays classic bid-ask looks like when you pick the right token and just let it sit.

the setup was simple. classic bid-ask, -42% range on 80 bins, single side sol. no overcomplicated strat, just a clean wide range deployed after the token already dumped around 30% from ath.

i never open a position at ath. ever.

opening at ath means you're the exit liquidity for everyone who bought earlier. waiting for the dump means you're entering when most people are panicking, and your range has real room to work both ways.

token selection for multi-days is different from quick plays. minimum 1M volume per 24 hours and mcap minimum 1M. anything below that and the token might just slowly bleed with no volume to generate fees.

chart quality matters too. i specifically avoid tokens that had a sudden vertical pump on day 1 of launch. those never survive multi-days. i want a token with a healthy chart, gradual price discovery, and volume that's been consistent, not a one-day wonder.

the classic -42% range means my position covers a lot of ground without needing to touch it. i set it and checked back occasionally, no active monitoring.

i still have 2 positions open on other pairs running the same approach. this march i'm leaning more into multi-days plays. the current market feels like a good spot for it. less noise, less fomo, just fees accumulating.

the r/r on multi-days right now is actually really clean if you pick the right token and be patient.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

i sat red for 3 hours on this position and didn't touch it. that patience just paid $65, 12% clean on shape-sol.

price moved against my range, fees weren't covering IL yet, and every instinct is telling you to close and cut the loss.

that's the whole point of this strat. you pick the right token, you set a default range, and you trust the setup. the exit pump came, swept through my bins, and i closed clean.

this is becoming my go-to when i don't have enough time to stare at charts for quick plays. with multidays classic bid-ask i check my position maybe twice a day. no active monitoring, no stress, just letting the range do its job.

the tradeoff is you have to be comfortable sitting in red. not every multi-days position is going to be green from the moment you open it. if you can't handle seeing IL on screen for a few hours without touching the position, this strat isn't for you.

but if you can hold, the exit pump almost always comes on a well-selected token. and when it does, it's a clean exit with fees stacked from the entire hold period.

slowly getting more comfortable with this approach. the r/r just makes sense for the current market.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy
